HomePage.seoTitle: Free PDF Tools Online: Compress, Merge, Split, Rotate, Convert
HomePage.seoDescription: Compress PDF online free, merge PDF files, split PDF pages, rotate, remove, or reorder PDF pages, convert PDF to JPG, and turn JPG to PDF in your browser.
HomePage.heroH1: Compress PDF Online Free and Convert Document Files Fast
HomePage.heroSubheading: Use free browser-first tools to compress PDF, merge PDF, split PDF, rotate, remove, or reorder PDF pages, convert PDF to JPG, and convert JPG to PDF for uploads, forms, and sharing.
HomePage.mockRoutingName: Choose compress, merge, split, or conversion based on the document task.
HomePage.mockRoutingSize: Start from workflow instead of guessing one tool.
HomePage.mockOutputName: Smaller PDFs, merged bundles, extracted page ranges, or converted image files.
HomePage.mockOutputSize: Built for uploads, attachments, and faster document prep.
HomePage.coreToolCompressCopy: Reduce PDF size with stronger modes for uploads, file limits, and scan-heavy documents.
HomePage.coreToolMergeCopy: Combine multiple PDF files into one document and export a single merged output.
HomePage.coreToolSplitCopy: Extract page ranges into smaller PDF parts for uploads, sharing, and follow-up compression.
HomePage.coreToolRotateCopy: Fix sideways or upside-down PDF pages before uploading, sending, or merging.
HomePage.coreToolRemoveCopy: Delete blank, duplicate, or unnecessary pages before uploading or sharing a PDF.
HomePage.coreToolReorderCopy: Move pages into the correct sequence before sending, merging, or compressing.
HomePage.coreToolPdfToJpgCopy: Turn PDF pages into JPG images for previews, uploads, and quick reuse.
HomePage.coreToolJpgToPdfCopy: Combine JPG or PNG images into one PDF for screenshots, receipts, and scanned bundles.
HomePage.coreToolsSectionTitle: Choose the tool path, not just a single upload box
HomePage.coreToolsSectionCopy: The site is structured around the core document actions people actually need: make one PDF smaller, combine many PDFs, split out needed pages, or move between PDF and JPG formats.
HomePage.workflowsSectionTitle: Built around common overseas document tasks
HomePage.workflowsSectionCopy: Search intent is fragmented. Some users want to compress a PDF for upload, some need to merge files, some need to split out only a few pages, and some need to convert between PDF and JPG. The homepage now works as the main hub across those search paths.
HomePage.wfColumnCompressionIntro: Start here when the main problem is upload limits, email attachments, or scanned files that need to shrink.
HomePage.wfColumnMergeIntro: Use merge when you need one final document for applications, invoices, supporting files, or archiving.
HomePage.wfColumnSplitIntro: Use split when the easiest way forward is exporting only the pages that actually matter.
HomePage.wfColumnConversionIntro: Use conversion when you need JPG images from PDF pages or one PDF from multiple screenshots or scans.
HomePage.toolPathUploadFirstCopy: Designed for portals and forms where the main goal is simply to make the file small enough to submit.
HomePage.toolPathResumeSafeCopy: A better fit for resumes, reports, and office PDFs that still need to stay readable.
HomePage.toolPathScannedRescueCopy: Focused on image-heavy documents, where size reduction is harder and browser-side compression has limits.
HomePage.moreCompressionPagesCopy: See long-tail pages for uploads, resumes, scanned PDFs, and attachment limits.
HomePage.mergePath1Copy: Combine resume, cover letter, certificates, and supporting files into one upload-ready PDF.
HomePage.mergePath2Title: Merge PDF for reports and invoices
HomePage.mergePath2Copy: Bundle separate client reports, invoices, or statements into one cleaner document.
HomePage.mergePath3Copy: Create one final PDF first, then compress it if the combined file is still too large.
HomePage.mergePath4Copy: Adjust the file order before generating a final merged document for sending or storage.
HomePage.splitPath1Copy: Use ranges like 1-3, 5, 7-9 to export only the pages you actually need.
HomePage.splitPath2Copy: Pull out just the required pages before uploading or attaching a smaller PDF.
HomePage.splitPath3Copy: Separate the large document into smaller parts, then compress only the pages you still need.
HomePage.splitPath4Copy: Break one multi-page file into smaller upload-ready documents for portals and systems.
HomePage.splitPath5Copy: Fix sideways pages before removing, reordering, merging, or compressing the final PDF.
HomePage.splitPath6Copy: Delete unnecessary pages first, then compress or submit a cleaner PDF.
HomePage.splitPath7Copy: Fix page sequence before sharing, merging, or compressing the final PDF.
HomePage.docWorkflow1Copy: Combine resume, cover letter, certificates, or supporting documents into one PDF before upload.
HomePage.docWorkflow2Copy: Merge separate PDFs into one cleaner file for sending, archiving, or client delivery.
HomePage.docWorkflow3Copy: Create one document first, then run compression if the final merged PDF is still too large.
HomePage.docWorkflow4Copy: Extract only the pages you need before emailing, uploading, or compressing a smaller document.
HomePage.docWorkflow5Copy: Rotate all pages or selected page ranges before submitting scanned PDFs.
HomePage.docWorkflow6Copy: Delete blank pages, duplicate covers, or extra instructions before uploading a cleaner PDF.
HomePage.docWorkflow7Copy: Move forms, resumes, certificates, or receipts into the right sequence before export.
HomePage.docWorkflow8Copy: Convert PDF pages to JPG files when a CMS, design flow, or preview system needs images instead of another PDF.
HomePage.docWorkflow9Copy: Turn JPG or PNG files into one PDF for receipts, scans, forms, and mobile document bundles.
HomePage.conversionLink1Copy: Export one JPG per PDF page for previews and quick reuse.
HomePage.conversionLink2Copy: Combine screenshots, receipts, or scans into one PDF.
HomePage.conversionLink3Copy: Useful when a page image is needed instead of another PDF.
HomePage.conversionLink4Copy: Build one upload-ready PDF from multiple image files.
HomePage.scenariosSectionTitle: Popular search pages for compression and conversion
HomePage.scenariosSectionCopy: These are the pages most likely to match the way people actually search: compress PDF online, convert PDF to JPG, convert JPG to PDF, and fix file-size problems for uploads or email.
HomePage.useCasesSectionTitle: Questions people search before choosing a tool
HomePage.useCasesSectionCopy: The same document problem can show up as an upload issue, a Gmail attachment limit, a preview image request, or a need to combine screenshots into one PDF.
HomePage.useCaseCopy: Choose the route that matches the job instead of forcing every visit into one generic PDF compression flow.
HomePage.directorySectionTitle: Browse by workflow, page type, or search-style route
HomePage.directorySectionCopy: This lower section behaves more like a tool directory: fast scanning, lots of entry points, and clear intent labels instead of heavy marketing copy.
HomePage.howItWorksStep1: Choose the exact task first: compress PDF, merge PDF, split PDF, convert PDF to JPG, or convert JPG to PDF.
HomePage.howItWorksStep2: Upload the file, choose the mode or route that matches the use case, and run the browser-side action.
HomePage.howItWorksStep3: Download the result, then move to the next tool if you still need compression, splitting, merging, or conversion.

SiteShell.footerIntro: Browser-first PDF and image tools for compressing, merging, splitting, and converting documents before upload, sharing, and submission.
SiteShell.footerMeta: No login. No install. Built for fast document tasks.
SiteShell.footerHighlight2: Merge multiple PDFs into one file
SiteShell.footerHighlight8: Turn JPG images into one PDF
SiteShell.footerTagline: Precision browser-first document tools for compression, merge, split, and conversion workflows.

UploadCard.headlineDescription: Upload one or more PDF files, choose a compression mode, and download smaller results.
UploadCard.heroEyebrow: Compression modes for real file limits
UploadCard.heroIntro: Pick the mode that fits your file: readability, uploads, tighter size limits, or scanned image-heavy documents.
UploadCard.heroPoint2Copy: Best for upload portals, attachments, and tighter document size limits.
UploadCard.metaDescription: Compress PDF online free in your browser with modes for readability, uploads, scanned documents, and tighter file size limits.
UploadCard.dropzoneHint: PDF only. Batch upload supported. Up to 50 MB per file in the current browser flow.
UploadCard.targetSizeMetaWithTarget: The browser will show whether the result fits your selected upload limit and suggest the next stronger retry when needed.
UploadCard.targetSizeMetaNoTarget: Use this when you only need the smallest practical browser-side result, not a fixed portal limit.
UploadCard.emptyText: Upload a PDF to see file size reduction, download actions, and stronger compression options.
UploadCard.compareHint: Compare before submitting this compressed file.
UploadCard.noTargetSelected: No fixed target size was selected for this compression run.
UploadCard.switchFiltersHint: Switch filters to see the rest of the compression queue.
UploadCard.queueNoticeAdded: {count, plural, =1 {# file added to the queue.} other {# files added to the queue.}}

errors.unsupportedFile: {fileName} is not a supported PDF file. Upload a .pdf document and try again.
errors.emptyFile: {fileName} is empty. Upload a PDF with actual document pages and try again.
errors.fileTooLarge: {fileName} is larger than 50 MB. Try a smaller PDF or split the document before compressing it in the browser.
errors.compressionFailedMessage: This PDF could not be processed in the current browser flow.
errors.compressionFailedHint: Try another compression mode, a smaller file, or split the document before compressing.
errors.protectedPdfMessage: {fileName} needs an open password. Browser compression cannot open it without that password.
errors.protectedPdfHint: If you know the open password, unlock it in a PDF reader first. FileSmaller’s “Remove restrictions” tool only helps when the file opens without a password (owner restrictions).
errors.emptyPdfHint: Use a PDF with actual document pages before trying compression.
errors.emptyPdfShortHint: Use a PDF that contains actual document pages.
errors.unreadablePdfMessage: {fileName} could not be read as a valid PDF.
errors.unreadablePdfHint: Try opening and re-exporting the file first, or upload a cleaner PDF copy.
errors.browserLimitMessage: {fileName} is large for an aggressive browser-side compression pass.
errors.browserLimitHint: Split the PDF first, or try Strong mode before running a deeper pass.
errors.compressionFailedFallbackHint: Try a different mode first. If the file is scan-heavy or image-heavy, Scanned PDF mode usually gives the strongest browser-side path.
errors.unsupportedFileHint: Upload a real .pdf file before starting compression.
errors.fileTooLargeHint: Split the file first or try a smaller PDF in the browser flow.
errors.browserNotSupportedMessage: This browser does not support the current PDF compression flow. Try a modern Chrome, Edge, Safari, or Firefox build.
errors.browserNotSupportedHint: Switch to a modern browser with FileReader and current PDF APIs enabled.
errors.previewNotAvailable: Preview rendering is only available in the browser.
errors.canvasNotAvailable: Canvas rendering is not available in this browser.

resultSummary.lightGood: Better readability with a lighter size reduction.
resultSummary.lightDefault: Light mode favors readability over aggressive shrinking.
resultSummary.balancedGood: Good default reduction for everyday PDF sharing.
resultSummary.balancedDefault: Balanced mode keeps a safer trade-off between size and clarity.
resultSummary.strongGood: Strong mode is working well for upload limits and attachments.
resultSummary.strongDefault: Strong mode is aimed at smaller uploads and tighter attachments.
resultSummary.extremeGood: Extreme mode is pushing toward the smallest browser-side result.
resultSummary.extremeDefault: Extreme mode is best when upload limits matter more than visual quality.
resultSummary.scannedGood: Scanned PDF mode is helping on image-heavy pages.
resultSummary.scannedDefault: Scanned PDF mode is tuned for image-heavy files that resist lighter compression.

qualityHint.portalLimitCopy: This path is tuned for strict size limits and may visibly reduce scan or image detail. Open the PDF once before submitting it.
qualityHint.balancedScanCopy: This default scanned path keeps more detail than the portal-limit path while still shrinking image-heavy pages strongly.
qualityHint.moderateScanCopy: This path reduces image-heavy pages while keeping a safer readability margin for scans and forms.
qualityHint.qualityAwareCopy: This scanned path is selected from multiple browser render settings based on the smallest practical result.

targetStatus.underTargetCopy: This result is {difference} below your selected upload limit.
targetStatus.overTargetCopy: Browser-side compression is best-effort and does not guarantee every target size.
targetStatus.nextTryStronger: Retry with a stronger mode (or Scanned PDF for image-heavy files).
targetStatus.nextSplit: Split the PDF into smaller parts, then compress each part.
targetStatus.nextBrowserLimit: Very strict limits (e.g. 200KB–1MB on scans) often need desktop tools beyond the browser.

queueHint.imageHeavyCopy: At least one processed file behaves like a scan or image-heavy PDF. Use Scanned PDF mode for the strongest browser-side reduction path.
queueHint.largePdfsCopy: Large PDFs are often scan-heavy or image-heavy. If the first pass barely moves them, rerun the affected files with Scanned PDF mode.
queueHint.strictTargetCopy: Light mode is unlikely to hit a tight size limit. Start with Balanced or Strong, then use the recommended retry for files that miss the target.

suggestedAction.tryScannedScan: This file behaves more like a scan. Scanned PDF mode is the best next step for image-heavy pages.
suggestedAction.tryScannedImage: Strong mode helped, but image-heavy files usually need the scanned path for a more aggressive browser-side pass.
suggestedAction.tryNextAboveTarget: This result is still above the {target} target. {mode} is the next practical retry.
suggestedAction.tryBalanced: Light mode preserved readability, but the file barely moved. Balanced mode is the safest stronger retry.
suggestedAction.tryStrong: Balanced mode did not reduce enough for tighter upload limits. Strong mode is the next practical step.
suggestedAction.tryExtreme: This result may still miss stricter upload caps. Extreme mode pushes harder toward the smallest browser-side result.
suggestedAction.tryStronger: If you still need a smaller file, {mode} is the next stronger mode in this compression path.

UploadCard.technicalHint: FileSmaller is using page count and bytes-per-page signals to decide whether this PDF behaves more like a clean office file, a mixed document, or a scan.
UploadCard.scannedHintTitle: Looks like a scanned or image-heavy PDF
UploadCard.scannedHintText: Larger bytes per page usually means this file is driven more by images than text.

light.label: Light
light.description: Keeps more visual quality with smaller structural cleanup.

balanced.label: Balanced
balanced.description: Default trade-off between readability and file size.

strong.label: Strong
strong.description: Pushes harder for smaller uploads and attachments.

extreme.label: Extreme
extreme.description: Uses the smallest practical rebuild path in the browser.
extreme.bestFor: Upload failures and tighter file limits

scanned.label: Scanned PDF
scanned.description: Aggressive cleanup path for image-heavy or scanned documents.

warnings.didNotShrink: This PDF did not shrink much in the browser. Image-heavy files may need a stronger server-side compressor.
warnings.scannedHeavyNotScanned: This file looks closer to a scan than a text PDF. Standard modes may not shrink it enough.
warnings.aggressiveQuality: Aggressive browser-side compression can reduce visual quality on image-heavy PDFs.
warnings.scannedModeSkipped: Scanned PDF mode was skipped because this file looks more like a {profileLabel}. FileSmaller used {effectiveModeLabel} instead.

recommendations.tryScannedForImageHeavy: This PDF looks image-heavy. Try Scanned PDF mode for a stronger browser-side reduction path.
recommendations.resistingBrowserCompression: This file is resisting browser-side compression. A server-side compressor may be needed for a much smaller result.
recommendations.alreadyLightClean: This PDF already looks structurally light. Try Balanced or Strong mode only if upload limits still block you.
recommendations.longImageHeavyNeedsStronger: Long image-heavy PDFs often need Extreme or Scanned PDF mode to get under stricter upload caps.
recommendations.shrankWellOnLight: This file shrank well even on Light mode. You may not need a stronger compression setting.

browserLimit.pathLimitCopy: This PDF reached the practical browser-side compression limit. Download the current result and try splitting the PDF into smaller files before compressing again.
browserLimit.resistingTitle: This PDF is resisting browser compression
browserLimit.resistingCopy: The browser compression path was not able to shrink this file meaningfully. Split the PDF into smaller parts first and try compressing each part separately.

MergePdfCard.metaDescription: Merge PDF files online free in your browser. Combine multiple PDF documents into one merged file and download it instantly.
MergePdfCard.heroHeading: Build one merged PDF from multiple files
MergePdfCard.heroIntro: Upload PDF files, set the right order, and download one combined document in the browser.
MergePdfCard.heroPoint1Copy: Move files up or down before creating the final merged document.
MergePdfCard.heroPoint2Copy: Combine documents without adding a server-side document queue to the MVP.
MergePdfCard.heroPoint3Copy: Pairs naturally with compression, split, and later PDF conversion tools.
MergePdfCard.heading: Merge PDF files
MergePdfCard.description: Upload multiple PDFs, reorder them, and merge everything into one file.
MergePdfCard.dropzoneHint: Upload at least two files to merge.
MergePdfCard.emptyText: Upload two or more PDF files to build one merged document in your chosen order.
MergePdfCard.nextStepCopy: Download the merged file now, or open Compress PDF next if the final document still feels too large for upload limits.
MergePdfCard.hintText: Merge the files in your chosen order. Add more PDF files to build one final merged document.

errors.mergeFailedMessage: The selected files could not be merged in the current browser flow.
errors.mergeFailedHint: Check the files and try again with valid PDFs.
errors.needMoreFilesHint: Add at least two PDF files before starting the merge.
errors.unsupportedFileHint: is not a supported PDF file.
errors.protectedPdfHint: appears to be password-protected or restricted. Unlock the PDF first.

SplitPdfCard.metaDescription: Split PDF files online free in your browser. Extract page ranges, create separate PDF files, and download them instantly.
SplitPdfCard.heroEyebrow: Pull out only the pages you need
SplitPdfCard.heroHeading: Split a large PDF into smaller documents
SplitPdfCard.heroIntro: Upload one PDF, choose page ranges, and export separate files for forms, uploads, or follow-up compression.
SplitPdfCard.heroPoint1Copy: Use formats like 1-3, 5, 7-9 to define the exact pages you want to export.
SplitPdfCard.heroPoint2Copy: Split the document locally in the browser without a server-side queue for the MVP.
SplitPdfCard.heroPoint3Copy: Extract only the needed pages first, then compress the smaller PDFs if upload limits still matter.
SplitPdfCard.eyebrow: Split one PDF into smaller files
SplitPdfCard.heading: Split PDF files
SplitPdfCard.description: Upload one PDF, choose page ranges, and export separate PDF files in the browser.
SplitPdfCard.dropzoneSubtext: or click to choose a file
SplitPdfCard.dropzoneHint: Use formats like 1-3, 5, 7-9. Up to 50 MB in the current browser flow.
SplitPdfCard.rangesHint: Use commas to separate multiple ranges. Example: 1-2, 4, 7-9.
SplitPdfCard.emptyText: Upload one PDF to split out selected pages for forms, uploads, or document sharing.
SplitPdfCard.nextStepCopy: Download the extracted files now, then use Compress PDF on any files that still need smaller sizes for uploads.
SplitPdfCard.splitHint: Split works best when you define the smallest page ranges you actually need. Extracted files are usually small enough for direct upload.

errors.splitFailedMessage: The file could not be split in the current browser flow.
errors.splitFailedHint: Check the page ranges and try again.
errors.unsupportedFileHint: is not a supported PDF file.
errors.emptyPdfHint: is empty. Upload a PDF with actual document pages.
errors.invalidRangesHint: One or more page ranges are not valid for this document.
errors.protectedPdfHint: appears to be password-protected or restricted.
errors.fileTooLargeHint: Split the file first or try a smaller PDF in the browser flow.
errors.noPdfSelectedHint: Upload a PDF file before trying to split pages.

RotatePdfCard.metaDescription: Rotate PDF pages online free in your browser. Turn all pages or selected pages 90, 180, or 270 degrees and download the fixed PDF.
RotatePdfCard.heroHeading: Rotate all pages or selected page ranges
RotatePdfCard.heroIntro: Upload one PDF, choose a rotation angle, and download a corrected file directly from the browser.
RotatePdfCard.heroPoint1Copy: Fix sideways scans, upside-down exports, or mixed-orientation documents.
RotatePdfCard.heroPoint2Copy: Leave the range blank for all pages, or rotate only pages like 1, 3-5.
RotatePdfCard.heroPoint3Copy: Rotate first, then remove, reorder, merge, or compress the final document.
RotatePdfCard.heading: Rotate PDF pages
RotatePdfCard.description: Upload one PDF, rotate all pages or selected pages, and download the fixed file.
RotatePdfCard.dropzoneSubtext: or click to choose a file
RotatePdfCard.dropzoneHint: Rotate all pages or selected ranges like 1, 3-5. Up to 50 MB.
RotatePdfCard.angleHint: Use 90 degrees for sideways scans, 180 degrees for upside-down pages.
RotatePdfCard.pagesHint: Leave blank to rotate the whole PDF, or use formats like 1, 3-5.
RotatePdfCard.emptyText: Upload a PDF to rotate sideways pages before submitting, merging, or compressing.
RotatePdfCard.rotatedHint: The fixed PDF is ready to download. Run compression next if file size still matters.

errors.rotateFailedMessage: The file could not be rotated in the current browser flow.
errors.rotateFailedHint: Check the rotation settings and try again.
errors.unsupportedFileHint: is not a supported PDF file.
errors.invalidRangeHint: The page range does not match any pages in this document.
errors.protectedPdfHint: appears to be password-protected or restricted.
errors.fileTooLargeHint: Split the file first or try a smaller PDF in the browser flow.
errors.noPdfSelectedHint: Upload a PDF file before trying to rotate pages.

RemovePdfPagesCard.metaDescription: Remove pages from a PDF online free in your browser. Delete blank, duplicate, or unwanted pages and download a cleaned PDF.
RemovePdfPagesCard.heroEyebrow: Keep only the pages that matter
RemovePdfPagesCard.heroHeading: Remove pages before uploading or sending a PDF
RemovePdfPagesCard.heroIntro: Upload one PDF, enter the pages you want to remove, and export a cleaner file directly in the browser.
RemovePdfPagesCard.heroPoint1Copy: Use formats like 2, 4-6, 9 to remove single pages or page ranges.
RemovePdfPagesCard.heroPoint2Copy: The current flow edits the PDF locally without a server-side document queue.
RemovePdfPagesCard.heroPoint3Copy: Remove cover sheets, blanks, or duplicate pages before compressing or submitting.
RemovePdfPagesCard.heading: Remove PDF pages
RemovePdfPagesCard.description: Upload one PDF, enter pages to remove, and download a cleaned PDF in the browser.
RemovePdfPagesCard.dropzoneSubtext: or click to choose a file
RemovePdfPagesCard.dropzoneHint: Remove pages like 2, 4-6, 9. Up to 50 MB in the browser flow.
RemovePdfPagesCard.removeHint: Use commas for multiple pages or ranges. The output must keep at least one page.
RemovePdfPagesCard.emptyText: Upload a PDF to delete blank pages, extra cover sheets, or pages you do not need.
RemovePdfPagesCard.removedHint: The cleaned PDF is ready to download. Run compression next if file size still matters.

errors.removeFailedMessage: The pages could not be removed in the current browser flow.
errors.removeFailedHint: Check the page selection and try again.
errors.unsupportedFileHint: is not a supported PDF file.
errors.invalidSelectionHint: One or more page numbers are not valid for this document.
errors.protectedPdfHint: appears to be password-protected or restricted.
errors.fileTooLargeHint: Split the file first or try a smaller PDF in the browser flow.
errors.noPdfSelectedHint: Upload a PDF file before trying to remove pages.

ReorderPdfCard.metaDescription: Reorder PDF pages online free in your browser. Type a new page order, rearrange PDF pages, and download the reordered file.
ReorderPdfCard.heroHeading: Rearrange PDF pages in the browser
ReorderPdfCard.heroIntro: Upload one PDF, type the output page order, and download a reordered document without installing a desktop editor.
ReorderPdfCard.heroPoint1Copy: Use formats like 3,1,2,4-6 to move pages into a new sequence.
ReorderPdfCard.heroPoint2Copy: The current version rearranges the PDF locally in your browser.
ReorderPdfCard.heroPoint3Copy: Reorder pages, remove extras, then compress or merge the final document.
ReorderPdfCard.heading: Reorder PDF pages
ReorderPdfCard.description: Upload one PDF, type the new page order, and download a reordered PDF in the browser.
ReorderPdfCard.dropzoneSubtext: or click to choose a file
ReorderPdfCard.dropzoneHint: Use page order like 3,1,2,4-6. Up to 50 MB in the browser flow.
ReorderPdfCard.orderHint: Use commas for exact order. Ranges expand forward, so 4-6 means pages 4, 5, 6.
ReorderPdfCard.emptyText: Upload a PDF to move pages into the order needed for applications, reports, or forms.
ReorderPdfCard.reorderedHint: The reordered PDF is ready to download. Compress it next if file size still matters.

errors.reorderFailedMessage: The pages could not be reordered in the current browser flow.
errors.reorderFailedHint: Check the page order and try again.
errors.unsupportedFileHint: is not a supported PDF file.
errors.invalidOrderHint: The page order does not match the pages in this document.
errors.protectedPdfHint: appears to be password-protected or restricted.
errors.fileTooLargeHint: Split the file first or try a smaller PDF in the browser flow.
errors.noPdfSelectedHint: Upload a PDF file before trying to reorder pages.

PdfToJpgCard.metaDescription: Convert PDF to JPG online free in your browser. Export one JPG per page for previews, uploads, sharing, and content workflows.
PdfToJpgCard.heroEyebrow: Export one JPG image per PDF page
PdfToJpgCard.heroHeading: Turn PDF pages into shareable image files
PdfToJpgCard.heroIntro: Upload a PDF, choose the JPG quality level, and download separate page images for previews, CMS uploads, or content reuse.
PdfToJpgCard.heroPoint1Copy: Each PDF page becomes a separate JPG file for reuse outside the PDF format.
PdfToJpgCard.heroPoint2Copy: Render the PDF locally in the browser without adding a server render queue to the MVP.
PdfToJpgCard.heroPoint3Copy: Pairs naturally with JPG to PDF for document-image round trips and quick visual extraction.
PdfToJpgCard.eyebrow: Convert PDF pages into JPG images
PdfToJpgCard.heading: PDF to JPG
PdfToJpgCard.description: Upload one PDF, render each page in the browser, and download JPG files.
PdfToJpgCard.dropzoneSubtext: or click to choose a file
PdfToJpgCard.dropzoneHint: Each page will be exported as a JPG image. Up to 50 MB in the current browser flow.
PdfToJpgCard.qualityWebDesc: Smaller JPG files for uploads, previews, and sharing.
PdfToJpgCard.qualityBalancedDesc: Good default output for most page-to-image conversions.
PdfToJpgCard.qualityHighDesc: Larger JPG files with better visual detail.
PdfToJpgCard.emptyText: Use this when you need page images for previews, CMS uploads, sharing, or extracting visuals from a PDF.
PdfToJpgCard.nextStepCopy: Download the JPG files now, or use a lower JPG output mode next time if you need smaller image files for web uploads.
PdfToJpgCard.pdfToJpgHint: PDF to JPG works best when you need page previews, quick visual extraction, or JPG files for a CMS upload flow.

errors.conversionFailedMessage: The PDF could not be converted to JPG in the current browser flow.
errors.conversionFailedHint: Try a smaller PDF or check that your browser supports the required PDF rendering APIs.
errors.unsupportedFileHint: is not a supported PDF file.
errors.emptyPdfHint: is empty. Upload a PDF with actual document pages.
errors.fileTooLargeHint: Split the file first or try a smaller PDF in the browser flow.
errors.unreadablePdfHint: could not be read as a valid PDF.
errors.noPdfSelectedHint: Upload a PDF file before converting.
errors.pageExportFailed: The browser could not export one of the PDF pages as JPG.
errors.canvasNotAvailable: Canvas rendering is not available in this browser.

JpgToPdfCard.metaDescription: Convert JPG to PDF online free in your browser. Combine multiple JPG or PNG files into one PDF document for sharing and submission.
JpgToPdfCard.heroEyebrow: Turn image batches into one PDF
JpgToPdfCard.heroHeading: Combine screenshots, scans, and photos into a single document
JpgToPdfCard.heroIntro: Upload the images in the order you want, create one PDF, and then compress that document later if the final file still needs to shrink.
JpgToPdfCard.heroPoint1Copy: Reorder images before export so the final PDF matches the reading flow you want.
JpgToPdfCard.heroPoint2Copy: Create the PDF locally in the browser without adding a server-side image queue to the MVP.
JpgToPdfCard.heroPoint3Copy: Useful for receipts, mobile screenshots, scans, and image bundles that need to become one PDF file.
JpgToPdfCard.eyebrow: Turn JPG images into one PDF
JpgToPdfCard.heading: JPG to PDF
JpgToPdfCard.description: Upload JPG or PNG files, set the page order, and export one combined PDF.
JpgToPdfCard.dropzoneHeading: Drop JPG or PNG files here
JpgToPdfCard.dropzoneHint: Every uploaded image becomes one PDF page in the final file.
JpgToPdfCard.fitOriginalDesc: Keep each image at its original dimensions on its own PDF page.
JpgToPdfCard.fitInsideDesc: Keep the full image visible and centered on each PDF page.
JpgToPdfCard.fillPageDesc: Fill the page area completely, which may crop some edges on wide or tall images.
JpgToPdfCard.emptyText: Use this when you need one PDF from multiple screenshots, receipts, scans, or exported JPG pages.
JpgToPdfCard.nextStepCopy: Download the PDF now, then use Compress PDF if you need a smaller upload-ready file afterward.

errors.jpgToPdfFailedMessage: The images could not be converted to PDF in the current browser flow.
errors.jpgToPdfFailedHint: Check that all files are valid JPG or PNG images and try again.
errors.unsupportedImageHint: is not a supported JPG or PNG file.

compress.label: Compress
compress.title: Compress PDF
compress.copy: Reduce file size for uploads, portals, attachments, and image-heavy scans.

merge.label: Merge
merge.title: Merge PDF
merge.copy: Combine multiple PDF files into one clean document before sending or compressing.

split.label: Split
split.title: Split PDF
split.copy: Extract page ranges from one PDF and export smaller files for uploads or follow-up compression.

rotate.label: Rotate
rotate.title: Rotate PDF
rotate.copy: Turn sideways or upside-down pages upright before submitting or merging.

remove.label: Remove
remove.title: Remove PDF pages
remove.copy: Delete blank, duplicate, or unnecessary pages before uploading a cleaner PDF.

reorder.label: Reorder
reorder.title: Reorder PDF pages
reorder.copy: Move pages into the right sequence before export, merge, or compression.

pdfToJpg.label: PDF to JPG
pdfToJpg.title: PDF to JPG
pdfToJpg.copy: Export one JPG per PDF page for previews, sharing, uploads, and visual extraction.

jpgToPdf.label: JPG to PDF
jpgToPdf.title: JPG to PDF
jpgToPdf.copy: Combine screenshots, scans, and image files into one PDF document in the browser.

watermark.label: Watermark
watermark.title: Watermark PDF
watermark.copy: Add text or image watermarks to PDF pages for branding, confidentiality, and document protection.

pageNumbers.label: Page #
pageNumbers.title: Page Numbers
pageNumbers.copy: Add visible page numbers to PDF documents for reports, manuals, and professional presentation.

unlock.label: Unlock
unlock.title: Unlock PDF
unlock.copy: Remove password protection and restrictions from PDF files for editing, merging, and sharing.

protect.label: Protect
protect.title: Protect PDF
protect.copy: Add password protection to PDF files for secure sharing, confidential documents, and privacy.

ToolPageTemplate.capabilityBody: Compression runs entirely in your browser. Target sizes (1MB, 500KB, etc.) are best-effort goals—not a guarantee. Password-protected PDFs cannot be opened without the password. Scanned or image-heavy files may need a stronger mode, splitting, or a desktop tool for very strict limits.

SplitPageTemplate.eyebrow: Page-range splitting for real document tasks
SplitPageTemplate.heroTitle1: Extract the exact pages you need
SplitPageTemplate.heroDesc1: Useful for forms, signatures, certificates, reports, and upload-ready subsets.
SplitPageTemplate.heroDesc2: Enter formats like 1-3, 5, 7-9 to export one or more smaller PDF files.
SplitPageTemplate.heroDesc3: Break one large file into smaller parts first, then compress only the pages that still need smaller sizes.

PrivacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
PrivacyPage.intro: FileSmaller is a browser-first PDF compression website. This page explains what information may be collected, how uploaded files are handled in the current version, and what will change if the product later adds server-side processing.
PrivacyPage.analyticsContent: We may collect basic website analytics such as page views, referrers, device type, country-level location, and high-level interaction data to understand how visitors use the site and which pages perform best.
PrivacyPage.fileHandlingContent: In the current version, compression is designed to run in the browser whenever possible. That means uploaded PDF files are processed on the device instead of being sent to our server for long-term storage. If a later version introduces server-side compression, this policy must be updated before that workflow is used in production.
PrivacyPage.disclaimerContent: We do not currently claim that every file is processed entirely offline, that files are never transmitted under any future workflow, or that the service is suitable for regulated or highly sensitive document handling without additional review.
PrivacyPage.cookiesContent: Analytics providers may use cookies or similar identifiers to measure site usage. Cloudflare and other infrastructure providers may also process request metadata needed to deliver the website securely and reliably.
PrivacyPage.updatesContent: This policy may be updated as the product changes. If file handling, storage, login systems, payments, or server-side document processing are added, the policy should be revised before those features go live.
PrivacyPage.contactContent: Add a production contact email before public promotion so users have a clear way to ask privacy or data-related questions.
PrivacyPage.seoDescription: Read the privacy policy for FileSmaller, including analytics, browser-side PDF handling, and future processing disclosures.

UnlockPdfCard.metaTitle: Remove PDF Restrictions Online Free | FileSmaller
UnlockPdfCard.metaDescription: Remove owner-level PDF restrictions in your browser. Does not crack open passwords — use when the PDF opens without a password but editing is blocked.
UnlockPdfCard.heading: Remove PDF restrictions
UnlockPdfCard.description: Strip owner-level restrictions (edit/print/copy) when the PDF can open without an open password. Does not unlock files that require a password you do not know.
UnlockPdfCard.heroHeading: Remove owner restrictions — not open passwords
UnlockPdfCard.heroIntro: If your PDF opens without typing a password but blocks editing or printing, this tool can rebuild an unrestricted copy in the browser. It cannot crack open-password protection.
UnlockPdfCard.heroPoint1Copy: Helps when the file opens freely but flags editing, printing, or copying as restricted.
UnlockPdfCard.heroPoint2Copy: If the PDF asks for an open password, you must unlock it with the password in a PDF reader first.
UnlockPdfCard.heroPoint3Copy: After restrictions are removed, use Compress, Merge, or other FileSmaller tools on the file.
UnlockPdfCard.capabilityNote: This tool does not recover forgotten open passwords. It only helps with owner-level restrictions on PDFs that already open without a password.
UnlockPdfCard.dropzoneHint: Works best on PDFs that open without a password
UnlockPdfCard.resultTitleNotEncrypted: No restrictions found — saved a clean copy

errors.passwordProtectedHint: This PDF needs a user/open password. FileSmaller cannot open it without that password. Unlock it in a PDF reader you trust, then come back.

PageNumbersCard.metaTitle: Add Page Numbers to PDF Online Free — FileSmaller
PageNumbersCard.metaDescription: Add page numbers to PDF online free. Choose position, font, starting number, and prefix — all in your browser with no install.
PageNumbersCard.heading: Add Page Numbers to PDF
PageNumbersCard.description: Number every page of your PDF with adjustable position, font, starting number, and prefix — right in your browser.
PageNumbersCard.heroHeading: Number your PDF pages before sharing
PageNumbersCard.heroIntro: Add visible page numbers for reports, contracts, manuals, and any document that needs better navigation. Choose from six positions and three font families.
PageNumbersCard.heroPoint1Copy: Place numbers at the bottom or top of each page, aligned left, center, or right.
PageNumbersCard.heroPoint2Copy: Set the starting number and add a text prefix like Page or Chapter before each number.
PageNumbersCard.heroPoint3Copy: Choose from Helvetica, Times Roman, or Courier — each with regular and bold variants.
PageNumbersCard.dropzoneHint: or click to select a PDF file

WatermarkCard.metaTitle: Add Watermark to PDF Online Free — FileSmaller
WatermarkCard.metaDescription: Add text or image watermarks to PDF pages online free. Stamp CONFIDENTIAL, DRAFT, or a logo on every page for branding and document protection.
WatermarkCard.heading: Watermark PDF
WatermarkCard.description: Add text or image watermarks to your PDF pages for branding, confidentiality, and document protection — all in your browser.
WatermarkCard.heroIntro: Add diagonal text labels like CONFIDENTIAL or DRAFT, or overlay a company logo as a watermark. Adjust size, opacity, rotation, and color to match your needs.
WatermarkCard.heroPoint1Copy: Type any text or upload a PNG/JPG logo to stamp every page.
WatermarkCard.heroPoint2Copy: Adjust font size, opacity, rotation angle, and color for the perfect watermark look.
WatermarkCard.heroPoint3Copy: Your PDF stays on your device. Watermarks are applied directly in the browser.
WatermarkCard.dropzoneHint: or click to select a PDF file
WatermarkCard.imageDropzoneHint: PNG or JPG — best with transparent background

ProtectPdfCard.metaTitle: Protect PDF with Password Online Free — FileSmaller
ProtectPdfCard.metaDescription: Protect PDF with password online free. Add password protection to PDF files for secure sharing — all in your browser with no install.
ProtectPdfCard.heading: Protect PDF with Password
ProtectPdfCard.description: Add password protection to PDF files so only people with the password can open them — all in your browser.
ProtectPdfCard.heroIntro: Upload a PDF, set a password, and download a protected copy. Your file stays on your device — no upload to any server. Uses RC4 128-bit encryption compatible with all major PDF readers.
ProtectPdfCard.heroPoint1Copy: Set a password and encrypt your PDF instantly — no account, no install, no upload required.
ProtectPdfCard.heroPoint2Copy: Protected PDFs open in Adobe Acrobat, Preview, Chrome, and any standard PDF viewer after entering the password.
ProtectPdfCard.heroPoint3Copy: If you know the password, use our Unlock PDF tool to remove protection and restore an unrestricted copy.
ProtectPdfCard.dropzoneHint: or click to select a PDF file
ProtectPdfCard.passwordHint: Use a mix of letters, numbers, and symbols. Share the password securely with intended recipients.

errors.passwordsDoNotMatch: Passwords do not match. Please re-enter.
